UNSW Business School INFS2822 Programming for Data Analytics

Lecture 10 Advanced Topics & Course Review Copyright • There are some file-sharing websites that specialise in buying and selling academic work to and from university students.

• If you upload your original work to these websites, and if another student downloads and presents it as their own either wholly or partially, you might be found guilty of collusion — even years after graduation.

• These file-sharing websites may also accept purchase of course materials, such as copies of lecture slides and tutorial handouts. By law, the copyright on course materials, developed by UNSW staff in the course of their employment, belongs to UNSW. It constitutes copyright infringement, if not academic misconduct, to trade these materials. Welcome to check out • INFS5720 Business Analytics Methods • INFS3822 Artificial Intelligence for Business Analytics (2026, Term 1) • INFS5705 Artificial Intelligence for Business Analytics in Practice Machine Learning for NLP • Natural Language Processing (NLP) is an interdisciplinary field of ⎻ Statistics ⎻ Computer Science (Artificial Intelligence) ⎻ Linguistics

• Research on the interaction between ⎻ Human – Human language (text & speech) ⎻ Computer – Computational representation

Source: revolveai  Example of ML Model in NLP: Topic 9

Modeling • Aims to analyze documents to find the underlying topics (contexts) of those documents • Commonly known algorithm: Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) • Applications ‐ Market Research and Trend Analysis ‐ Legal Document Analysis ‐…  Past: How did Voice Assistants 10

Work? • Speech Recognition: converts the audio input into text

• Intent Recognition: identifies the user's intent

• Query Processing: converts the intent into a structured query that can be used to retrieve relevant information or perform specific actions

• Task Execution: executes the task associated with the intent, such as turning on smart home devices, fetching information from the internet

• Response Generation: speaks the response aloud to the user or present it as text on the screen  New Era: Pre-trained Large Language 11

Models Large-scale language models (e.g., Copilot or GPT) trained on vast corpora of text, leading to breakthroughs in many NLP tasks below, through transfer learning. • Question Answering: Creating systems that can understand natural language questions and provide relevant and accurate answers

• Sentiment Analysis: Analyzing text to determine the sentiment or emotion expressed by the writer, which has applications in social media monitoring, customer feedback analysis, and market research.

Data Exploration and Manipulation

Python Python Python Python fundamentals 1 fundamentals 2 fundamentals 3 fundamentals 4

Week 6 Week 7 Week 8 Week 9 Week 10

Recharge week Data Insights File Operations Machine Learning Advanced Topics Basics with Python Course Review Python Fundamentals 1 and 2 • Print and assignment statement • Data value • Variables • Data types (integer, float, string, Boolean)

• Arithmetic operators • Relational operators • Logical operators • String operators Python Fundamentals 3 Data structures • Tuple: A tuple is an immutable sequence in Python. Once a tuple is created, its elements cannot be changed, added, or removed.

• List: A list is a mutable, ordered sequence of elements. Elements in a list can be of different types and can be modified after creation.

• Set: A set is an unordered collection of unique elements. Sets are mutable, but elements must be immutable and unique.

• Dictionary: A dictionary is a mutable, unordered collection of key-value pairs. Keys must be unique and immutable (e.g., strings, numbers, tuples), but values can be of any type. Comparison of Data Structures

Mutable Ordered Indexing Duplicate Data

Tuple × List

Set × × × Dictionary 1 1 × 1 Since Python 3.7, dictionaries are order-preserving, that is they now behave like collections.OrderedDicts. There is no dedicated method to index into keys and values of the dictionary *Python Fundamentals 4 • Conditional statements ⎻ used to execute different blocks of code based on certain conditions ⎻ if-else • Loops ⎻ used for iterating over a sequence ⎻ break vs. continue statement ⎻ try-except blocks • Functions ⎻ a way to group code into reusable blocks ⎻ The parameters can take arguments (values) and can return values. (Predefined functions; User defined functions) Data Exploration and Manipulation

• Course Review

• Final Exam ⎻ Practice Questions during Lecture Final Exam • Date and time: Tuesday 9rd December 2024, 9:45 – 12:00 pm (AEST) • Duration: 2-hour writing time + 15 minutes reading time • Venue: UNSW Campus • Format: Multiple-choice questions (80%) and coding (20%) using Inspera Safe Mode • Content: Lecture, lab, individual assignment, group assignment, and quizzes, except the Interactive Widgets in Week 7, File Operations about JSON files in Week 8. • What you have to bring: Your laptop/macbook + power cable. Bring your physical UNSW Student ID card. If you don’t have one, a driver’s license or passport (physical copy only) is also accepted. • No course-related materials is allowed during the exam • Please click this website for further information related to what you can bring to the exam room: https://www.student.unsw.edu.au/exam/checklist Final Exam Format • Total number of questions to be answered – 36. ⎻ Multiple-Choice Questions (with only one correct answer) – 44 Marks • Each question is worth 2 marks. 22 questions. ⎻ Multiple-Choice Questions (with one or more correct answers) – 36 marks • Each question is worth 3 marks. 12 questions. ⎻ Coding Questions – 20 marks • One question worth 15 marks, the other worth 5 marks. 2 questions.  Multiple Choice Question 27

(Only One Correct Answer) • Instructions ⎻ Each question is worth 2 marks. ⎻ There is only one correct answer in each question and no negative mark for a wrong answer.

If you are NOT sure the correct answer, please choose one answer.  Multiple Choice Question 28

(Only One Correct Answer) – Inspera

After choosing your answer  Q1: What does the following expression return?

A. True B. False C. None D. SyntaxError  Q2: What is the output of the following code snippet?

A. True B. False C. None D. SyntaxError  Q3: What will be the output?

A. {3} B. {1, 2, 3, 4, 5} C. {} D. TypeError  Q4: What will be the output of the print statement?

A. 10 B. 20 C. 60 D. KeyError  Q5: What will be the output of the following code?

A. Odd Even Odd Even B. Even Odd Even Odd C. Even Odd Even Odd Even D. Odd Even Odd Even Odd 33  Multiple Choice Question 34

(One or More Correct Answers) • Instructions ⎻ Each question is worth 3 marks. Each question may have one or more correct answers. For each question, • Each question has four options. • If you select all correct answer(s), 3 marks are awarded. • If you select some but not all correct answer(s), partial marks are awarded. • If you choose an incorrect answer, some marks will be deducted. • The minimum score is capped at 0 mark. In other words, even though you select all wrong answers, the score is 0 and never be negative

If you are NOT sure the correct answer(s), please choose only one answer. Multiple Choice Question (One or More Correct Answer) – Inspera

After choosing your answer  Q6: Given a tuple below, which of the following option will you choose to update the value of the 2nd place to 8.

A. my_tuple{2} = 8 (-3 marks) B. my_tuple[2] = 8 (-3 marks) C. my_tuple[1] = 8 (-3 marks) D. None of them (+3 marks)  Q7: After running the following code snippet, what are the output of k and s?

A. k = 3 (-1.5 marks) B. k = 2 (+1.5 marks) C. s = 16 (+1.5 marks) D. s = 12 (-1.5 marks)  Q8: Which of the following will return True?

A. 6 < 12 (+1 mark) B. 30 == 30.00 (+1 mark) C. “80 == 80” (-1.5 marks) D. 0 == False (+1 mark)

Skills in the MCQ (with One or More Correct Answers) Note: The minimum score is capped at 0 mark. In other words, even though you select all wrong answers, the score is 0 and never be negative.

What if choosing some correct answer(s) and/or a wrong answer? • Choosing A and B – You will get 2 marks. • Choosing A, B and C – You will get 0.5 marks. • Choosing A and C – You will get -0.5 marks, but the minimum cap for each question is 0. So, you will get 0.

If you are NOT sure the correct answer(s), please choose only one answer. Coding – Inspera Coding • Creating a Pandas Series with 10 values, initiating all values as 0 • Replace the third element with 86. • Print the first four elements in this series. Coding – Solution • Creating a Pandas Series with 10 values, initiating all values as 0 • Replace the third element with 86. • Print the first four elements in this series.

import pandas as pd s = pd.Series(0, index = range(10)) s[2] = 86 print(s[:4])  Inspera

Inspera • Inspera is the examination platform you will use. • You will use Safe Exam Browser (SEB) for your exam: Inspera will stop you from accessing other applications and/or websites on your laptop while SEB is running. This will ensure that you cannot look at your notes or access the websites. • You must download and install the SEB application. You can access this website to download Inspera and test (discuss on the next slide) prior to the examination if you have not already done so: • Inspera: Information for Students | UNSW Current Students  Student Practice Test • The advice we got from the Inspera team is for all of you to complete the Inspera Student Practice Test (https://unsw.inspera.com/student#list/112563919). This is an open test that any UNSW student can take to check whether they have successfully downloaded SEB. This Practice Test will also help students prepare their laptops for opening a test in SEB. It is your responsibility to make sure the SEB is downloaded and works before coming to class. If you have any questions, please go to see UNSW IT walk-in services (Contact Us | UNSW IT | UNSW Sydney). • You can find the generic password on: https://www.student.unsw.edu.au/exams/inspera/oncampus

• My recommendation is to have a test on SEB again on Monday, 08, December, the day before the exam, to ensure everything is working.  Updates and Upgrades • Both Windows and Mac have regular updates and upgrades

• Do not trigger updates or upgrades two days before the examination to ensure the settings are not modified. I believe this might be one of the causes for some students who had temporary access issues in the second quiz

• If you did, then you should perform the Student Practice Test (https://unsw.inspera.com/student#list/112563919) again  Last Remark • It is your responsibility to double-check everything before and during the exam. We will not accept unreasonable or insubstantial excuses, such as claiming that a laptop battery ran out during an exam. • Read the instructions carefully before attempting the questions • Remember, the end time of the exam is controlled by Inspera, it will stop automatically when time is up. So, you must keep an eye on your time.

• Lastly, Good luck! Thank You and All the Best!
